title: History Quiz
subtitle: The Joseon Dynasty & The American Revolution
search_scope: Joseon Dynasty Korea history American Revolution
primer:
This quiz spans two pivotal chapters in world history — one from East Asia, one from the Americas — that share a surprising theme: the birth of something new from something old.

The Joseon Dynasty (1392–1910) was one of the longest-ruling dynasties in world history, governing the Korean peninsula for over 500 years. Founded by General Yi Seong-gye — who became King Taejo — after overthrowing the Goryeo Kingdom, Joseon was built on the principles of Neo-Confucianism, which shaped Korean society, governance, and culture for centuries. Among its greatest legacies is Hangul, the Korean alphabet created by King Sejong the Great, which allowed common people to become literate for the first time.

The American Revolution (1775–1783) was the colonial uprising that gave birth to the United States of America. What began with the shots fired at Lexington and Concord escalated into a full-scale war for independence. Key figures like George Washington, the turning point at the Battle of Saratoga, the signing of the Declaration of Independence in 1776, and the final British surrender at Yorktown all shaped a new nation founded on the ideals of liberty and self-governance.

trivia_questions.question: Who was the founder of the Joseon dynasty and what was his position?
trivia_questions.explanation: Yi Seong-gye, also known as King Taejo, founded the Joseon dynasty in 1392.

curated_sources.title: Taejo of Joseon — World History Encyclopedia
curated_sources.description: Biography of Yi Seonggye and the founding of the Joseon Dynasty in 1392.

curated_sources.title: Joseon Dynasty — Britannica
curated_sources.description: Encyclopedic overview of the dynasty's founding, key rulers, and historical significance.

trivia_questions.question: Is the year the Joseon dynasty began odd or even?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Joseon dynasty began in 1392, which is an even number.

curated_sources.title: Joseon Dynasty — Britannica
curated_sources.description: Overview of the Joseon Dynasty including its founding year of 1392.

trivia_questions.question: During the American Revolution, which battle was considered the turning point?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Battle of Saratoga (1777) convinced France to support the American cause.

curated_sources.title: Battle of Saratoga — Britannica
curated_sources.description: How the American victory at Saratoga in 1777 convinced France to enter the war.

curated_sources.title: Battle of Saratoga — American Battlefield Trust
curated_sources.description: Detailed account of the two battles of Saratoga and their impact on the Revolution.

trivia_questions.question: Which of the following is true about the Battles of Lexington/Concord?
choices: Paul Revere was the only rider who warned the militias that British troops were coming.
The Battles of Lexington and Concord marked the beginning of the American Revolutionary War.
Historians are certain who fired the first shot at Lexington.
trivia_questions.correct_answer: The Battles of Lexington and Concord marked the beginning of the American Revolutionary War.
trivia_questions.explanation: These battles on April 19, 1775, were the first military engagements of the American Revolution.

curated_sources.title: Battles of Lexington and Concord — Britannica
curated_sources.description: Overview of the opening battles of the American Revolution on April 19, 1775.

trivia_questions.question: Who commanded the Continental Army during the American Revolution?
trivia_questions.explanation: George Washington was appointed Commander-in-Chief of the Continental Army in 1775.

curated_sources.title: George Washington — Commander-in-Chief — Britannica
curated_sources.description: How Washington led the Continental Army through eight years of war to victory.

trivia_questions.question: Which document officially declared the American colonies independent from Britain?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Declaration of Independence, adopted on July 4, 1776, formally declared independence from Britain.

curated_sources.title: Declaration of Independence — National Archives
curated_sources.description: The full text and history of the Declaration of Independence adopted on July 4, 1776.

trivia_questions.question: Which battle effectively ended major fighting in the American Revolution?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Siege of Yorktown in 1781 forced British General Cornwallis to surrender, ending major hostilities.

curated_sources.title: Siege of Yorktown — Britannica
curated_sources.description: How the combined American and French forces trapped Cornwallis and ended the war.

trivia_questions.question: Which of the following are prime factors of the year the American Revolution began?
trivia_questions.explanation: The American Revolution began in 1775. Its prime factorization is 5 × 5 × 71, so the prime factors are 5 and 71.

curated_sources.title: American Revolution — Britannica
curated_sources.description: Overview of the American Revolution, which began in 1775.

trivia_questions.question: How many years did the Joseon dynasty last?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Joseon dynasty lasted from 1392 to 1910, which is 518 years.

curated_sources.title: Joseon Dynasty — World History Encyclopedia
curated_sources.description: Full history of the Joseon Dynasty from its founding in 1392 to its end in 1910.
